evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix with Blues colormap, labeled axes (true/predicted), bold diagonal annotations, and colorbar clearly conveys the classifier performance claim.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 in script; pdffonts confirms Type 3 fonts (DejaVuSans, DejaVuSans-Bold) — text is not selectable or editable; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blues sequential colormap is perceptually uniform and colorblind-safe; appropriate for count data; no red/green directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: 7x6 in figure with tight_layout is reasonable; 8pt cell annotation text is small for 100 cells but legible; colorbar well-placed with fraction/pad tuning.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes rows=true/columns=predicted, mentions bold diagonal, Blues colormap, and gives specific diagonal range (69–92); white border highlights on diagonal are a nice touch.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 fonts (confirmed by pdffonts); script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is bitmapped glyphs, not selectable vector text.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (44K), figure.png (60K), caption.tex (433B), gen_figure.py (1.8K); bonus figure.svg also generated.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific numerical finding (diagonal counts 69–92), explains row/column semantics, notes bold formatting, and describes colormap — meets publication standard.

overall_strengths: Palette and colormap choice (Blues) is exemplary: perceptually uniform, colorblind-safe, and semantically appropriate for count data.
Caption is publication-ready with a concrete numerical claim, axis semantics, and colormap explanation; all required output files are present.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0): PDF embeds Type 3 bitmapped glyphs instead of TrueType/Type 42, making text non-selectable and non-editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
Default DejaVu Sans font family is used throughout; a professional sans-serif (e.g. Helvetica, Arial, or a system font via rcParams) would improve typographic quality.
